title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Haberdasher discusses the recent misfortune of Elizabeth "Black Beth" Johnston with Sally Finch. Sally explains her intention to leave the residence due to a series of unsettling incidents, including a cut scarf, a tampered backpack, and disappearing shoes; she expresses suspicion that someone "not ordinary" is living in the house.

Mrs. Haberdasher then moves to the student common room, where she finds Valerie Hobhouse, Nigel Chapman, Patricia Lane, and a frightened girl in a raincoat named Celia. Mrs. Haberdasher informs Nigel that someone used green ink to sabotage Elizabeth Johnston's essay, noting that Nigel is known to use that color. Nigel denies the accusation, stating that Elizabeth is a role model to him, but he discovers his own ink bottle is nearly empty. Nigel subsequently threatens Celia, questioning her alibi, though she claims she was at the hospital all day. Valerie suggests the situation has escalated beyond mere pranks and requires action.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot examines a silver evening shoe on Baker Street, which confirms his suspicions. Miss Lemon provides Poirot with a letter from her sister, Mrs. Haberdasher, and facilitates a phone call between them. During the call, Mrs. Haberdasher mentions that the house is currently quiet and notes that an explorer named Miss Bartlett recently gave a lecture. Poirot arranges to visit the residence that evening to deliver a lecture on detective theories and criminal cases. A notice regarding the lecture is posted in the common room. Following dinner, Mrs. Haberdasher introduces the elderly, black-haired Mr. Poirot to the students.
